skill.name: google-drive-automation
skill.description: Automate Google Drive file operations (upload, download, search, share, organize) via Rube MCP (Composio). Upload/download files, manage folders, share with permissions, and search across drives.
skill.summary: Documentation for automating Google Drive workflows through Composio's Rube MCP toolkit
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 163 static analysis findings are false positives from markdown code blocks. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code. Backtick patterns (152 findings) are markdown code spans for API tool names, not Ruby/shell commands. Crypto algorithm flags (10 findings) are legitimate API parameter names. The single URL is documented configuration for Rube MCP endpoint. No security risks present.

content.value_statement: Manually managing Google Drive files through the web interface is slow and error-prone. This skill provides comprehensive documentation for automating file operations, folder organization, sharing, and searches through the Rube MCP toolkit.
actual_capabilities: Upload and download files from Google Drive with proper MIME type handling
Search files using Google Drive query syntax with filters for name, type, date, and folder
Create and organize folder structures with proper parent ID references
Manage file sharing and permissions with role-based access control
List and browse shared drives with pagination support
Move files between folders while handling parent relationships
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active Google Drive OAuth authentication
Upload tool requires files to be in internal S3 storage before upload (s3key parameter)
Basic upload limited to 5MB files; larger files require resumable upload workflow
Google Workspace files require specific MIME type parameters for export; native files must omit this parameter
Shared drive access depends on OAuth scopes; insufficient permissions cause 403 errors

use_cases.title: Automate File Backup Workflows
use_cases.description: Upload local files to Google Drive folders automatically, organize by date or project, and verify successful transfers with metadata checks.
use_cases.target_user: Data manager backing up project files

use_cases.title: Search and Organize Large Drives
use_cases.description: Find specific files across multiple folders using Drive query syntax, then reorganize into structured folder hierarchies with proper parent references.

use_cases.title: Manage Access Permissions
use_cases.description: Share files with users or groups, set appropriate reader/writer roles, and audit permissions using list and update operations.
use_cases.target_user: Team lead controlling document access for collaborative projects

prompt_templates.title: Upload a File to Google Drive
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload the file [filename] to my Google Drive in the folder named [folder name]. The file is located at [local path].
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to upload a single document to a specific folder

prompt_templates.title: Search for Files by Type and Date
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Google Drive for all [file type] files modified after [date]. Show me the results with file names and modification dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to find recent files of a specific format

prompt_templates.title: Create Organized Folder Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a folder named '[project name]' in my Google Drive root, then create subfolders named '[subfolder1]', '[subfolder2]', and '[subfolder3]' inside it.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to set up a new project directory structure

prompt_templates.title: Share File with Custom Permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: Share the file named '[filename]' with [email address] giving them [role] access. Verify the permission was set correctly.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to grant specific access to a collaborator

output_examples.input: Find all PDF files in my Drive that were modified this week
output_examples.output: Found 12 PDF files modified in the last 7 days. Here are the results: Q4-Financial-Report.pdf (modified 2 days ago), Invoice-1042.pdf (modified 3 days ago), Contract-Draft.pdf (modified 5 days ago), etc.

output_examples.input: Upload report.docx to my Projects folder
output_examples.output: Successfully uploaded report.docx to the 'Projects' folder. File ID: 1a2B3c4D5e6F7g8H. Upload completed at 2:34 PM.

output_examples.input: Create a folder structure for the new marketing campaign
output_examples.output: Created folder 'Q2-Marketing-Campaign' in root. Created 3 subfolders inside: 'Assets', 'Copy', and 'Reports'. All folders verified and ready for use.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Use GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE to resolve folder names to IDs before creating child folders or moving files
Set MIME type parameter only when exporting Google Workspace formats; omit it for native file downloads
Follow nextPageToken in search results until exhausted to ensure complete data retrieval
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Google Drive operations
anti_patterns: Passing folder names directly to parent_id parameter instead of resolving to folder IDs first
Setting mime_type parameter when downloading native files (PDF, images, Office docs), which causes errors
Creating complex queries with more than 5-10 OR clauses, which exceed Drive query complexity limits
Using wildcards with asterisks in name searches instead of the contains operator

faq.question: Why does my file upload fail with 's3key required'?
faq.answer: The upload tool requires files to be in internal S3 storage first. Use the Rube MCP storage tools to upload your local file and obtain an s3key, then pass that key to GOOGLEDRIVE_UPLOAD_FILE.

faq.question: How do I find the folder ID for my target folder?
faq.answer: Use GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE with a query like 'name contains "Projects"' and set the folder scope. The response will include the folder ID you can use as parent_id.

faq.question: When should I set the mime_type parameter?
faq.answer: Only set mime_type when downloading Google Workspace files (Docs, Sheets, Slides) to export them to other formats. Omit this parameter for native files like PDFs, images, or Office documents.

faq.question: Why do I get 403 PERMISSION_DENIED errors?
faq.answer: This usually means your OAuth connection lacks sufficient scopes for shared drives. Re-authenticate with the RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS tool and ensure you grant the requested permissions.

faq.question: What is the difference between GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE and GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_FILES?
faq.answer: GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE uses Drive query syntax for flexible searching across your entire Drive. GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_FILES browses contents within a specific folder when you already know the folder ID.

faq.question: How do I move a file to a different folder?
faq.answer: Use GOOGLEDRIVE_MOVE_FILE with the file_id, add_parents (destination folder ID), and remove_parents (source folder ID) parameters. Both must be folder IDs, not names.
